neighborhoods.name: Waterfront / Financial District
neighborhoods.vibe: Polished, professional, fast-paced. Toronto's business hub meets lakefront living.
neighborhoods.vibe_fr: Professionnel, dynamique. Le centre des affaires de Toronto au bord du lac.
neighborhoods.description: Toronto's Financial District is the city's economic heart, home to Canada's largest banks and Bay Street. The Waterfront area offers stunning lake views, the scenic harbourfront trail, and easy access to the PATH underground network.
best_for: Students at UofT or George Brown Waterfront campus
Anyone who wants transit access to everywhere
neighborhoods.safety_description: Well-lit, heavily patrolled business district with 24/7 foot traffic

neighborhoods.name: Queen West / Entertainment District
neighborhoods.vibe: Creative, trendy, artsy. Toronto's cultural heartbeat with galleries, indie shops, and nightlife.
neighborhoods.vibe_fr: Creatif, branche, artistique. Le coeur culturel de Toronto.
neighborhoods.description: Queen West is consistently ranked among the world's coolest neighborhoods. It's a hub for art galleries, independent boutiques, live music venues, and some of Toronto's best restaurants. The Entertainment District offers TIFF, theatres, and nightlife.
best_for: Anyone who wants to be in Toronto's cultural center
neighborhoods.safety_description: Busy, well-populated neighborhood with active street life day and night

neighborhoods.name: Downtown Core / Eaton Centre
neighborhoods.vibe: Central, convenient, bustling. You're at the crossroads of everything Toronto has to offer.
neighborhoods.vibe_fr: Central, pratique, anime. Au carrefour de tout ce que Toronto offre.
neighborhoods.description: The Downtown Core is Toronto's most central neighborhood, anchored by the massive CF Eaton Centre mall, Yonge-Dundas Square, and easy subway access in every direction. TMU campus is a 5-minute walk away.
best_for: Anyone who wants maximum transit access
Budget-conscious residents (lowest starting price at The Yonge)
neighborhoods.safety_description: Busy intersection area with heavy foot traffic and TTC security presence

neighborhoods.name: Garden District / Cabbagetown
neighborhoods.vibe: Quiet, residential, green. A hidden gem with Victorian charm and the most affordable co-living in Toronto.
neighborhoods.vibe_fr: Calme, residentiel, verdoyant. Un joyau cache au charme victorien.
neighborhoods.description: The Garden District and Cabbagetown are among Toronto's oldest and most charming neighborhoods. Tree-lined streets, Victorian houses, and Allan Gardens make this a peaceful residential area that's still close to downtown action.
best_for: Anyone who prefers quiet, residential living over downtown bustle
neighborhoods.safety_description: Residential neighborhood with active community watch and well-lit streets along main corridors
